A net laying ship, also known as a net layer, net tender, gate ship or boom defence vessel was a type of naval auxiliary ship.
A net layer's primary function was to lay and maintain steel anti-submarine nets or anti torpedo nets.
objects.artist: Photographer not identified. Credit National Defence

objects.title: Box
objects.extract: A box (plural: boxes) is a container with rigid sides used for the storage or transportation of its contents. Most boxes have flat, parallel, rectangular sides (typically rectangular prisms).

objects.title: Bottle
objects.description: bottle at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 1975.1.1591)

objects.title: British Army
objects.description: Land warfare force of the United Kingdom
objects.extract: The British Army is the land warfare force of the United Kingdom responsible for defending the UK, the British Overseas Territories and Crown Dependencies. The British Army, founded in 1707, has seen involvement in most of the world's major wars throughout history, including both world wars.

objects.title: Azurite-CMG-NM
objects.description: Sanple of azurite found in Capiapo, Atacama, Chile

objects.title: Russula aeruginea
objects.extract: Russula aeruginea, also known as the green brittlegill, grass-green russula, the tacky green russula, or the green russula, is an edible Russula mushroom. Widely distributed in northern temperate regions, it is usually found under birch, mostly in pine forests.

objects.title: Sausage
objects.extract: A sausage is a type of meat product usually made from ground meat—often pork, beef, or poultry—along with salt, spices and other flavourings. Other ingredients, such as grains or breadcrumbs, may be included as fillers or extenders.

objects.title: Salvia officinalis
objects.extract: Salvia officinalis, common sage or sage, is a perennial, evergreen subshrub, with woody stems, grayish leaves, and blue to purplish flowers. It is a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the Mediterranean region, though it has been naturalized in many places throughout the world.

objects.title: Hummingbird
objects.extract: Hummingbirds are birds native to the Americas and comprise the biological family Trochilidae. With approximately 375 species and 113 genera, they occur from Alaska to Tierra del Fuego, but most species are found in Central and South America.

objects.title: Dragon
objects.extract: A dragon is a mythical creature found in the folklore of cultures throughout the world. It is roughly serpentine in form, and often possesses features drawn from different animals, such as wings and claws.

objects.title: Besra
objects.extract: The besra  (Tachyspiza virgata), also called the besra sparrowhawk, is a bird of prey in the family Accipitridae. It was formerly placed in the genus Accipiter.

objects.title: Train
objects.extract: A train (from Old French trahiner, from Latin trahere, "to pull, to draw") is a series of connected vehicles that run along a railway track and transport people or freight. Trains are typically pulled or pushed by locomotives (often known simply as "engines"), though some are self-propelled, such as multiple units or railcars.

objects.title: Totem pole
objects.description: Monumental carvings by Indigenous peoples of the Pacific Northwest
objects.extract: Totem poles (Haida: gyáaʼaang) are monumental carvings found in western Canada and the northwestern United States. They are a type of Indigenous Northwest Coast art, consisting of poles, posts or pillars, carved with symbols or figures.

objects.title: Steel and tin cans
objects.description: Sealed container for storage of foods
objects.extract: A steel can, tin can, tin (especially in British English, Australian English, Canadian English and South African English), or can is a  container made of thin metal, for distribution or storage of goods. Some cans are opened by removing the top panel with a can opener or other tool; others have covers removable by hand without a tool.

objects.title: Western wear
objects.description: American clothing style associated with the Old West and cowboy culture
objects.extract: Western wear is a category of men's and women's clothing which derives its unique style from the clothes worn in the 19th century Wild West. It ranges from accurate historical reproductions of American frontier clothing, to the stylized garments popularized by Western film and television or singing cowboys such as Gene Autry and Roy Rogers in the 1940s and 1

objects.title: Steaming
objects.extract: Steaming is a method of cooking using steam. In steaming, the food is fully immersed in steam, with no air present, so that the steam condenses on the food, and then the condensate drips down away from the food.

objects.title: Tractor
objects.description: Engineering vehicle specifically designed to deliver a high tractive effort
objects.extract: A tractor is an engineering vehicle specifically designed to deliver a high tractive effort (or torque) at slow speeds, for the purposes of hauling a trailer or machinery, such as that used in agriculture, mining or construction. Most commonly, the term is used to describe a farm vehicle that provides the power and traction to mechanize agricultural tasks, e

objects.title: Sergeant Reckless
objects.description: U.S. Marine pack horse and Dickin Medal recipient
objects.extract: Staff Sergeant Reckless (c. 1948 – May 13, 1968), a decorated warhorse who held official rank in the United States military, was a mare of Mongolian horse breeding. Out of a racehorse dam, she was purchased in October 1952 for $250 (equivalent to $3,000 in 2025) from a Korean stableboy at the Seoul racetrack who needed money to buy an artificial leg for his

objects.title: Iranian cuisine
objects.extract: Iranian cuisine comprises the culinary traditions of Iran. Due to the historically common usage of the term "Persia" to refer to Iran in the Western world, it is alternatively known as Persian cuisine, despite Persians being only one of a multitude of Iranian ethnic groups who have contributed to Iran's culinary traditions.

objects.title: Griddle
objects.extract: A griddle, also called a girdle in the UK, is a cooking device consisting mainly of a broad, usually flat cooking surface. Nowadays it can be either a movable metal pan- or plate-like utensil, a flat heated cooking surface built onto a stove as a kitchen range, or a compact cooking machine with its own heating system attached to an integrated griddle acting

objects.title: Scaffolding
objects.description: Temporary structure used to support a work crew and materials
objects.extract: Scaffolding, also called scaffold or staging, is a temporary structure used to support a work crew and materials to aid in the construction, maintenance and repair of buildings, bridges and all other human-made structures. Scaffolds are widely used on site to get access to heights and areas that would be otherwise hard to get to.

objects.title: Samovar
objects.description: Metal container used to heat and boil water
objects.extract: A samovar (Russian: самовар, IPA: [səmɐˈvar] , lit. 'self-brewer') is a metal container traditionally used to heat and boil water. Although originating in Russia, the samovar is well known outside of the country and has spread through Russian culture to other parts of Eastern Europe, as well as West and Central and South Asia.
objects.artist:
Ivorymammoth at English Wikipedia

(Original text: Dimitri)

objects.title: Tubaphone
objects.extract: A tubaphone is a type of metallophone constructed from a series of metal tubes arranged in a keyboard configuration. The tubes are similar in length to that of a xylophone, and sound vaguely like a glockenspiel.

objects.title: Minivan
objects.description: Type of van designed for private use
objects.extract: A minivan, commonly known as a van, is a car classification for vehicles designed to transport passengers in the rear seating row(s), with reconfigurable seats in two or three rows. The equivalent classification in Europe is MPV (multi-purpose vehicle), people carrier, or M-segment.

objects.title: Dusky-legged Guan
objects.extract: The dusky-legged guan (Penelope obscura) is a species of bird in the family Cracidae, the chachalacas, guans, and curassows. It is found in Uruguay, northeastern Argentina and southernmost areas of Paraguay and Brazil.

objects.title: Wombat
objects.description: Species of marsupial native to Australia
objects.extract: Wombats are short-legged, muscular quadrupedal marsupials of the family Vombatidae that are native to Australia. Living species are about one metre (40 in) in length with small, stubby tails and weigh between 20 and 35 kg (44 and 77 lb).

objects.title: Minivan
objects.description: Type of van designed for private use
objects.extract: A minivan, commonly known as a van, is a car classification for vehicles designed to transport passengers in the rear seating row(s), with reconfigurable seats in two or three rows. The equivalent classification in Europe is MPV (multi-purpose vehicle), people carrier, or M-segment.

objects.title: Massage
objects.description: Manual kneading of the body's soft tissues
objects.extract: Massage is the rubbing or kneading of the body's soft tissues. Massage techniques are commonly applied with hands, fingers, elbows, knees, forearms, feet, or a device.
objects.artist: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers Europe District from Wiesbaden, Germany

objects.title: Walters Art Museum
objects.description: Art museum in Baltimore, Maryland, US
objects.extract: The Walters Art Museum is a public art museum located in the Mount Vernon neighborhood of Baltimore, Maryland. Founded and opened in 1934, it holds collections from the mid-19th century that were  amassed substantially by major American art and sculpture collectors, including William Thompson Walters and his son Henry Walters.

objects.title: Strapping
objects.description: Fastening a strap around item or bundle
objects.extract: Strapping, also known as bundling and banding, is the process of applying a strap to an item to combine, stabilize, hold, reinforce, or fasten it. A strap may also be referred to as strapping.

objects.title: Barnacle
objects.extract: Barnacles are arthropods of the subclass Cirripedia in the subphylum Crustacea. They are related to crabs and lobsters, with similar nauplius larvae.

objects.title: Ancient Greece
objects.description: Greek civilization from 1200 BC to 600 AD
objects.extract: Ancient Greece (Ancient Greek: Ἑλλάς, romanized: Hellás) was a northeastern Mediterranean civilization, existing from the Greek Dark Ages of the 12th–9th centuries BC to the end of classical antiquity (c. 600 AD), comprising a loose collection of culturally and linguistically related city-states and communities. Prior to the Roman period, most of these regio

objects.title: List of cabbage dishes
objects.extract: This is a list of cabbage dishes and foods. Cabbage (Brassica oleracea or variants) is a leafy green or purple biennial plant, grown as an annual vegetable crop for its dense-leaved heads.

objects.title: Corrugated fiberboard
objects.extract: Corrugated fiberboard, corrugated cardboard, or corrugate is a type of paper packaging material consisting of a fluted corrugated sheet and one or two flat linerboards. It is used for making corrugated boxes.

objects.title: Lists of foods
objects.description: Categorically organized list of food items
objects.extract: This is a  categorically organized list of foods. Food is any substance consumed to provide nutritional support for the body.

objects.title: Logging truck
objects.extract: A logging truck or timber lorry is a large truck used to carry logs. Some have integrated flatbeds, some are discrete tractor units, and some are configured to spread a load between the tractor unit and a dollied trailer pulled behind it.

objects.title: Conveyor belt
objects.description: Close-loop rubber band used in motorized conveying system
objects.extract: A conveyor belt is the carrying medium of a belt conveyor system (often shortened to a belt conveyor). A belt conveyor system consists of two or more pulleys (sometimes referred to as drums), with a closed loop of carrying medium—the conveyor belt—that rotates about them.

objects.title: USS Charlton Hall
objects.description: Cargo steamship that served in the US Navy in World War I
objects.extract: USS Charlton Hall (ID-1359) was a cargo steamship that was built in Scotland in 1907 and served in the merchant fleets of the United Kingdom, United States and Greece. She served in the United States Navy in 1918.
objects.artist: U.S. Naval Historical Center photograph; entire collection is in the public doma

objects.title: Statue of Liberty
objects.description: Colossal sculpture in New York Harbor
objects.extract: The Statue of Liberty (Liberty Enlightening the World; French: La Liberté éclairant le monde) is a colossal neoclassical sculpture of a robed and crowned woman on Liberty Island, part of New York City, in New York Harbor. The copper-clad statue, a gift to the United States from the people of France, was designed by French sculptor Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi,

objects.title: Chrysuronia oenone
objects.extract: The golden-tailed sapphire (Chrysuronia oenone) is a species of hummingbird in the family Trochilidae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela.

objects.title: Playground slide
objects.description: Slides for children to play on in playgrounds
objects.extract: Playground slides are found in parks, schools, playgrounds and backyards. The slide is an example of the simple machine known as the inclined plane, which makes moving objects up and down easier, or in this case more fun.

objects.title: Wedding ensemble
objects.description: wedding ensemble (American) by Herman Rossberg (MET, C.I.68.53.5a–h)

objects.title: Wright brothers
objects.description: American aviation pioneers, inventors of the airplane
objects.extract: The Wright brothers, Orville Wright (August 19, 1871 – January 30, 1948) and Wilbur Wright (April 16, 1867 – May 30, 1912), were American aviation pioneers generally credited with inventing, building, and flying the world's first successful airplane. They made the first controlled, sustained flight of an engine-powered, heavier-than-air aircraft with the Wri

objects.title: List of Iranian foods
objects.extract: This is a list of Iranian foods and dishes. Iranian cuisine (Persian cuisine) comprises the cooking traditions of Iran.

objects.title: Drill bit
objects.extract: A drill bit is a cutting tool used with a drill to remove material and create holes, typically with a circular cross-section. Drill bits are available in various sizes and shapes, designed to produce different types of holes in a wide range of materials.

objects.title: Laser
objects.description: Device that emits light via optical amplification
objects.extract: A laser is a device that emits light through a process of optical amplification based on the stimulated emission of electromagnetic radiation. The word laser originated as an acronym for light amplification by stimulated emission of radiation.

objects.title: Bucket hat
objects.description: Cloth hat with a downward-sloping brim
objects.extract: A bucket hat (variations of which include the fisherman's hat, Irish country hat and session hat) is a hat with a narrow, downward-sloping brim.  Typically, the hat is made from heavy-duty cotton fabric such as denim or canvas, or heavy wool such as tweed, sometimes with metal eyelets placed on the crown of the hat for ventilation.

objects.title: Tambourine
objects.extract: The tambourine is a musical instrument in the percussion family consisting of a frame, often of wood or plastic, with pairs of small metal jingles, called "zills". Classically the term tambourine denotes an instrument with a drumhead, though some variants may not have a head.

objects.title: Furniture
objects.description: Objects used to support human activities
objects.extract: Furniture refers to objects intended to support various human activities, such as seating (e.g., stools, chairs, and sofas), eating (tables), storing items, working, and sleeping (e.g., beds and hammocks). Furniture is also used to hold objects at a convenient height for work (as horizontal surfaces above the ground, such as tables and desks), or to store th

objects.title: Baby transport
objects.extract: Various methods of transporting children have been used in different cultures and times. These methods include baby carriages (prams in British English), infant car seats, portable bassinets (carrycots), strollers (pushchairs), slings, backpacks, baskets and bicycle carriers.

objects.title: Gao-Guenie meteorite
objects.extract: Gao–Guenie is a H5 ordinary chondrite meteorite that fell on Burkina Faso, West Africa, on March 5, 1960. The fall was composed of many fragments and it is one of the largest observed meteorite showers in Africa to date.

objects.title: Lists of foods
objects.description: Categorically organized list of food items
objects.extract: This is a  categorically organized list of foods. Food is any substance consumed to provide nutritional support for the body.

objects.title: John Lennon
objects.extract: John Winston Ono Lennon (born John Winston Lennon; 9 October 1940 – 8 December 1980) was an English musician, songwriter and activist. He gained global fame as the founder, co-lead vocalist and rhythm guitarist of the Beatles.

objects.title: Milan Fashion Week
objects.extract: Milan Fashion Week (Italian: Settimana della moda di Milano) is a clothing trade show held semi-annually in Milan, Italy. Upcoming autumn/winter fashions are showcased in February/March of each year, and upcoming spring/summer fashions are showcased in September/October of each year.

objects.title: Golf equipment
objects.description: Items used to play the sport of golf
objects.extract: Golf equipment encompasses the various items that are used to play the sport of golf.  Types of equipment include the golf ball, golf clubs, golf divot tool, golf ball marker and devices that aid in the sport.

objects.title: H. R. Giger
objects.extract: Hans Ruedi Giger ( GHEE-gər; German: [ˈɡiːɡɐ]; 5 February 1940 – 12 May 2014) was a Swiss artist best known for his airbrushed images that blended human physiques with machines, an art style known as "biomechanical". He was part of the special effects team that won an Academy Award for the visual design of Ridley Scott's 1979 sci-fi horror film Alien, and wa

objects.title: Wooden fish
objects.extract: A wooden fish, also known as a muyu, moktak, mogeo, mokugyo, Chinese temple block, or wooden bell is a type of woodblock that originated from China that is used as a percussion instrument by monks and lay people in the Mahayana tradition of Buddhism.  They are used in Buddhist ceremonies in China, Korea, Japan, Vietnam and other Asian countries.

objects.title: Horse racing
objects.extract: Horse racing is an equestrian performance activity, typically involving two or more horses ridden by jockeys (or sometimes driven without riders) over a set distance for competition. It is one of the most ancient of all sports, as its basic premise – to identify which of two or more horses is the fastest over a set course or distance – has been mostly unchan

objects.title: Alexander Sarcophagus
objects.extract: The Alexander Sarcophagus is a late 4th century BC Hellenistic stone sarcophagus from the Royal necropolis of Ayaa near Sidon, Lebanon. It is adorned with high relief carvings of Alexander the Great and scrolling historical and mythological narratives.

objects.title: Atari
objects.extract: Atari () is a brand name that has been owned by several entities since its inception in 1972. It is currently owned by French holding company Atari SA (formerly Infogrames) and its focus is on "video games, consumer hardware, and licensing".

objects.title: Audio equipment
objects.description: Devices that reproduce, record, or process sound
objects.extract: Audio equipment refers to devices that reproduce, record, or process sound. This includes microphones, radio receivers, AV receivers, CD players, tape recorders, amplifiers, mixing consoles, effects units, headphones, and speakers.

objects.title: Drill bit
objects.extract: A drill bit is a cutting tool used with a drill to remove material and create holes, typically with a circular cross-section. Drill bits are available in various sizes and shapes, designed to produce different types of holes in a wide range of materials.
objects.artist: Original uploader was Bushytails at en.wikipedia

objects.title: Academic dress
objects.extract: Academic dress is a traditional form of clothing for academic settings, mainly tertiary (and sometimes secondary) education, worn mainly by those who have obtained a university degree (or similar), or hold a status that entitles them to assume them (e.g., undergraduate students at certain old universities). It is also known as academical dress, academicals,

objects.title: Phoenicia
objects.extract: The Phoenicians were an ancient Semitic people who inhabited city-states in Canaan along the Levantine coast of the eastern Mediterranean, primarily in present-day Lebanon and parts of coastal Syria. Their maritime civilization expanded and contracted over time, with its cultural core stretching from Arwad to Mount Carmel.

objects.title: Two-man saw
objects.description: Saw designed for use by two sawyers
objects.extract: A two-man saw (known colloquially as a "misery whip") is a saw designed for use by two sawyers. While some modern chainsaws are so large that they require two persons to control, two-man crosscut saws were primarily important when human power was used.

objects.title: Horse racing
objects.extract: Horse racing is an equestrian performance activity, typically involving two or more horses ridden by jockeys (or sometimes driven without riders) over a set distance for competition. It is one of the most ancient of all sports, as its basic premise – to identify which of two or more horses is the fastest over a set course or distance – has been mostly unchan

objects.title: Skunk
objects.description: Common name of mammals in the family Mephitidae
objects.extract: Skunks are mammals in the family Mephitidae. They are known for their ability to spray a liquid with a strong, unpleasant scent from their anal glands.

objects.title: Wi-Fi
objects.extract: Wi-Fi () is a family of wireless network protocols based on the IEEE 802.11 family of standards, which are commonly used for local area networking of devices and Internet access, allowing nearby digital devices to exchange data by radio waves. These are the most widely used computer networks, used globally in home and small office networks to link devices an

objects.title: Optical disc packaging
objects.description: Case or other packaging used to protect optical discs
objects.extract: Optical disc packaging is the packaging that accompanies compact discs, DVDs, and other formats of optical discs. Most packaging is rigid or semi-rigid and designed to protect the media from scratches and other types of exposure damage.

objects.title: Bioplastic
objects.description: Plastics derived from renewable biomass sources
objects.extract: Bioplastics are plastic materials produced from renewable biomass sources. In the context of bioeconomy and the circular economy, bioplastics remain topical.

objects.title: Ribs (food)
objects.description: Cut of meat with bones from the ribs of an animal
objects.extract: Ribs of pork, beef, lamb, and venison are a cut of meat.  The term ribs usually refers to the less meaty part of the chops, often cooked as a slab (not cut into separate ribs).

objects.title: Dioptase-J Altmann 53626
objects.description: sample of dioptase found in Renéville, Congo-Brazzaville

objects.title: Pocket bottle
objects.description: pocket bottle at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 1980.502.39)

objects.title: Lacquerware
objects.extract: Lacquerware are objects decoratively covered with lacquer. Lacquerware includes small or large containers, tableware, miscellaneous lacquered small objects carried by people, and larger objects such as furniture and even coffins painted with lacquer.
objects.artist: Richard Mortel from Riyadh, Saudi Arabia

objects.title: White wine
objects.extract: White wine is a wine that is fermented without undergoing the process of maceration, which involves prolonged contact between the juice and the grape skins, seeds, and pulp. The wine color can be straw-yellow, yellow-green, or yellow-gold.

objects.title: Filigree
objects.description: Form of intricate metalwork used in jewellery
objects.extract:
Filigree (also less commonly spelled filagree, and formerly written filigrann or filigrene) is a form of intricate metalwork used in jewellery and other small forms of metalwork.
In jewellery, it is usually of gold and silver, made with tiny beads or twisted threads, or both in combination, soldered together or to the surface of an object of the same metal a

objects.title: Toxopsiella perplexa
objects.extract: Toxopsiella perplexa is a species of Cycloctenidae spider endemic to New Zealand.
objects.artist: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa

objects.title: Gebrüder Thonet
objects.extract: Gebrüder Thonet, or the Thonet Brothers, was a European furniture manufacturer. Three firms descended from the original company remain active today: Thonet GmbH in Germany, Thonet Vienna in Austria, and TON in the Czech Republic.

objects.title: Service life
objects.description: Period of time where an object can fulfill a function
objects.extract: A product's service life is its period of use in service. Several related terms describe more precisely a product's life, from the point of manufacture, storage, and distribution, and eventual use.

objects.title: Sushi
objects.extract: Sushi (すし, 寿司, 鮨, 鮓; pronounced [sɯɕiꜜ] or [sɯꜜɕi] ) is a traditional Japanese dish made with vinegared rice (鮨飯, sushi-meshi), typically seasoned with sugar and salt, and combined with a variety of ingredients (ねた, neta), such as seafood, vegetables, or meat; raw seafood is the most common, although some may be cooked. While sushi has numerous styles and pr

objects.title: Rat
objects.extract: Rats are various medium-sized, long-tailed rodents. Species of rats are found throughout the order Rodentia, with the genus Rattus containing the most familiar rats.

objects.title: Liquid-crystal display
objects.description: Display that uses the light-modulating properties of liquid crystals
objects.extract: A liquid-crystal display (LCD) is a flat-panel display or other electronically modulated optical device that uses the light-modulating properties of liquid crystals combined with polarizers to display information. Liquid crystals do not emit light directly but instead use a backlight or reflector to produce images in color or monochrome.

objects.title: Team RC
objects.description: Brazilian motor racing team, based in Curitiba
objects.extract: RC Competições / Team RC, currently competing as Eurofarma RC, is a Brazilian auto racing team owned by Rosinei Campos and based in Curitiba, Paraná. It currently competes in the Stock Car Pro Series and Império Endurance Brasil.

objects.title: Hinge
objects.extract: A hinge is a mechanical bearing that connects two solid objects, typically allowing only a limited angle of rotation between them. Two objects connected by an ideal hinge rotate relative to each other about a fixed axis of rotation, and all other translations or rotations are prevented; thus a hinge has one degree of freedom.

objects.title: Malakoff
objects.extract: A malakoff is a ball of fried cheese typically found in western Switzerland, particularly in the villages of Eysins, Begnins, Bursins, Luins, and Vinzel (where it is also known as “Vinzel beignet”) along the shores of Lake Leman. The name comes from the Battle of Malakoff, the dish itself having been invented by Swiss mercenaries during the siege of Sevastop

objects.title: Tunnel boring machine
objects.extract: A tunnel boring machine (TBM), also known as a "mole" or a "worm", is a machine used to excavate tunnels. TBMs are an alternative to drilling and blasting methods and "hand mining", allowing more rapid excavation through hard rock, wet or dry soil, or sand (although each requires specialized TBM technologies).
objects.artist: The original uploader was Tambo at Hungarian Wikipedia.

objects.title: Halogen lamp
objects.extract: A halogen lamp (also called tungsten halogen, quartz-halogen, and quartz iodine lamp) is an incandescent lamp consisting of a tungsten filament sealed in a compact transparent envelope that is filled with a mixture of an inert gas and a small amount of a halogen, such as iodine or bromine. The combination of the halogen gas and the tungsten filament produces

objects.title: Opel
objects.description: German automotive brand, subsidiary of Stellantis
objects.extract: Opel Automobile GmbH (German pronunciation: [ˈoːpl̩]), usually shortened to Opel, is a German automobile manufacturer which has been a subsidiary of Stellantis since 16 January 2021. It was owned by the American automaker General Motors from 1929 until 2017 and the PSA Group prior to its merger with Fiat Chrysler Automobiles to form Stellantis in 2021.

objects.title: Cornet
objects.description: instrument in the collections of the Swedish Museum of Performing Arts (F105)

objects.title: History of England
objects.description: Hungarian book about the English history from the end of the 19th century in four volumes

objects.title: Nudibranch
objects.extract: Nudibranchs () are a group of soft-bodied marine gastropod molluscs, belonging to the order Nudibranchia, that shed their shells after their larval stage. They are noted for their often extraordinary colours and striking forms, and they have been given colourful nicknames to match, such as "clown", "marigold", "splendid", "dancer", "dragon", and "sea rabbit"

objects.title: Addo Elephant National Park
objects.description: Wildlife conservation park near Gqeberha in the Eastern Cape, South Africa
objects.extract: Addo Elephant National Park (AENP) is a diverse wildlife conservation park situated close to Gqeberha in South Africa and is one of the country's 20 national parks. It currently ranks third in size after Kruger National Park and the Kgalagadi Transfrontier Park.

objects.title: Wolf spider
objects.extract:
Wolf spiders are members of the family Lycosidae (from Ancient Greek  λύκος (lúkos) 'wolf'), named for their robust and agile hunting skills and excellent eyesight.
They live mostly in solitude, hunt alone, and usually do not spin webs.

objects.title: Lees (fermentation)
objects.description: Deposits of dead yeast in wine-making
objects.extract: Lees are sedimentary deposits of dead yeast or residual yeast and other particles that precipitate, or are carried by the action of "fining", to the bottom of a vat of wine after fermentation and aging. When brewing beer, this sediment is known as trub, not lees.

objects.title: Milkshake
objects.extract: A milkshake (sometimes simply called a shake) is a sweet beverage made by blending milk, ice cream, and flavorings or sweeteners such as butterscotch, caramel sauce, chocolate syrup, or fruit syrup into a thick, sweet, cold mixture. It may also be made using a base made from non-dairy products, including plant milks such as almond milk, coconut milk, or soy

objects.title: Helios
objects.description: Greek god and personification of the Sun
objects.extract: In ancient Greek religion and mythology, Helios (; Ancient Greek: Ἥλιος pronounced [hɛ̌ːlios], lit. 'Sun'; Homeric Greek: Ἠέλιος) is the god who personifies the Sun. His name is also Latinized as Helius, and he is often given the epithets Hyperion ("the one above") and Phaethon ("the shining").

objects.title: Calcite-MGL 79410
objects.description: sample of calcite found at Guiyang, Hunan, China

objects.title: Fish knife
objects.description: Table knife used for eating fish
objects.extract: The fish knife together with fish fork represent a set of utensils specialized for eating fish. A fish knife is a strange-looking, purposely blunt implement.

objects.title: Dethatcher
objects.description: Device that removes thatch from lawns
objects.extract: A dethatcher or lawn scarifier is a device that removes thatch from lawns. Types of dethatchers include motorized dethatchers or those that can be pulled behind a garden tractor.

objects.title: Meatball
objects.description: Dish of ground meat rolled into a ball
objects.extract: A meatball is ground meat (mince) rolled into a ball, sometimes along with other ingredients, such as bread crumbs, minced onion, eggs, butter, and seasoning. Meatballs are cooked by frying, baking, steaming, or braising in sauce.

objects.title: Bangle
objects.extract: A bangle is a traditionally rigid bracelet which is usually made of metal, wood, glass or plastic. These ornaments are worn mostly by women in the India, Southeastern Asia, the Arabian Peninsula, and Africa.

objects.title: Chime bar
objects.extract: A chime bar or resonator bell is a percussion instrument consisting of a tuned metal bar similar to a glockenspiel bar, with each bar mounted on its own wooden resonator. Chime bars are played with mallets again similar to a glockenspiel.

objects.title: Boulengerula taitana
objects.extract: Boulengerula taitana (common names: Taita African caecilian, Taita Hills caecilian, Taita Mountains caecilian) is a species of caecilian. It is endemic to the Taita Hills region of southeast Kenya.

objects.title: Potato planter
objects.extract:
A potato planter is a farm implement for sowing seed potatoes.
Hand potato planters, often referred to as foot-operated planters, are long-handled tools attached to a hinged "beak".

objects.title: Bowfin
objects.extract: The ruddy bowfin (Amia calva) is a ray-finned fish native to North America. Common names include mudfish, mud pike, dogfish, grindle, grinnel, swamp trout, and choupique.

objects.title: First aid kit
objects.description: Collection of supplies and equipment that is used to give medical treatment
objects.extract: A first-aid kit is a collection of supplies and equipment used to provide immediate medical care, known as first aid, for minor injuries and emergencies until professional treatment becomes available. Kits are assembled for a wide range of settings, and their contents vary according to anticipated risks, the training level of users, local regulations, and ap

objects.title: Georgian era
objects.description: Historical period in Britain from 1714 to c. 1830–37
objects.extract: The Georgian era was a period in British history from 1714 to 1830, named after the Hanoverian kings George I, George II, George III and George IV. The definition of the Georgian era is also often extended to include the relatively short reign of William IV, which ended with his death in 1837. The subperiod that is the Regency era is defined by the regency o

objects.title: Enamelled glass
objects.description: Glass which has been decorated with vitreous enamel
objects.extract: Enamelled glass or painted glass is glass which has been decorated with vitreous enamel (powdered glass, usually mixed with a binder) and then fired to fuse the glasses. It can produce brilliant and long-lasting colours, and be translucent or opaque.

objects.title: Flatbread
objects.extract: A flatbread is bread typically made with flour, water, and salt, with or without leavening, which are mixed and rolled into flattened dough. They are usually baked quickly at high temperatures and range from below one millimeter to a few centimeters in thickness.

objects.title: Field hockey stick
objects.description: Means by which field hockey is played
objects.extract: In field hockey, each player carries a stick and cannot take part in the game without it. The stick for an adult is usually in the range 89–95 cm (35–37 in) long.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Martin ZigZag assumed (based on copyright c

objects.title: Japanese curry
objects.extract: Japanese curry (カレー, karē) is commonly served in three main forms: curry rice (カレーライス, karē raisu) (curry over white rice), curry udon (カレーうどん, karē udon) (curry over thick noodles), and curry bread (カレーパン, karē pan) (a curry-filled pastry). It is one of the most popular dishes in Japan.

objects.title: Creampot
objects.description: cream pot at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 23.80.20)

objects.title: GI glasses
objects.description: Eyeglasses issued by the American military to its service members
objects.extract: GI glasses are eyeglasses issued by the American military to its service members. Dysphemisms for them include the most common "birth control glasses" (BCGs) and other variants.

objects.title: Chelsea boot
objects.description: Close-fitting ankle boot with elastic panels
objects.extract: Chelsea boots are close-fitting, ankle-length boots with elastic side panels, a low heel and a snug fit around the ankle. They often have a loop or tab of fabric on the back of the boot, enabling the boot to be pulled on.

objects.title: Beer in Iran
objects.extract: The earliest known chemical evidence of beer in the world dates to c. 3500–3100 BC from the site of Godin Tepe in the Zagros Mountains of western Iran, and there is evidence of beer-drinking over a long period in the Median Empire.

objects.title: Rufous-throated Bronze Cuckoo
objects.extract: The rufous-throated bronze cuckoo (Chalcites ruficollis) is a species of cuckoo in the family Cuculidae. It was formerly placed in the genus Chrysococcyx.

objects.title: Bell-bottoms
objects.description: Trousers cut wider at the ankle than the knees
objects.extract: Bell-bottoms (or flares) are a style of trousers that become wider from the knees downward, forming a bell-like shape of the trouser leg.

objects.title: Porcelain
objects.extract: Porcelain (), also called china, is a ceramic material made by heating raw materials, generally including kaolinite, in a kiln to temperatures between 1,200 and 1,400 °C (2,200 and 2,600 °F). The greater strength and translucence of porcelain, relative to other types of pottery, arise mainly from vitrification and the formation of the mineral mullite within

objects.title: Bicycle parking
objects.extract: Bicycle parking is part of the cycling infrastructure of a populated place allowing for the storage of bicycles when they are not being used. Parking facilities for bicycles include racks, lockers, parking stations, and covered areas.

objects.title: Batik
objects.extract: Batik is a dyeing technique using wax resist. The term is also used to describe patterned textiles created with that technique.

objects.title: Portable engine
objects.description: Early engine which could be easily moved between work sites
objects.extract: A portable engine is an engine, either a steam engine or an internal combustion engine, that sits in one place while operating (providing power to machinery), but (unlike a stationary engine) is portable and thus can be easily moved from one work site to another. Mounted on wheels or skids, it is either towed to the work site or moves there via self-propulsi

objects.title: Pliers
objects.extract: Pliers are a hand tool used to hold objects firmly, possibly developed from tongs used to handle hot metal in Bronze Age Europe. They are also useful for bending and physically compressing a wide range of materials.
objects.artist:
Ed Stevenhagen at Dutch Wikipedia

Later versions were uploaded by Siebrand at n

objects.title: Rocking horse
objects.extract: A rocking horse is a child's toy, usually shaped like a horse and mounted on rockers similar to a rocking chair. There are two sorts, the one where the horse part sits rigidly attached to a pair of curved rockers that are in contact with the ground, and a second sort, where the horse hangs on a rigid frame by iron straps.

objects.title: Casio
objects.extract: Casio Computer Co., Ltd. (カシオ計算機株式会社, Kashio Keisanki Kabushiki-gaisha) is a Japanese multinational electronics manufacturing corporation headquartered in Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan.

objects.title: Inkwell
objects.extract: An inkwell is a small jar or container, often made of glass, porcelain, silver, brass, or pewter, used for holding ink in a place convenient for the person who is writing. The artist or writer dips the brush, quill, or dip pen into the inkwell as needed or uses the inkwell as the source for filling the reservoir of a fountain pen.

objects.title: Agenoria
objects.description: Early steam locomotive built by the Foster, Rastrick and Co partnership of Stourbridge
objects.extract: The Agenoria is an early steam locomotive built by the Foster, Rastrick and Co partnership of Stourbridge, England. It first ran on 2 June 1829 along the Kingswinsford Railway which was a 3-mile-long (4.8 km) line linking mines in the Shut End area of the Black Country with a canal basin at Ashwood on the Staffordshire and Worcestershire Canal.

objects.title: Disposable cup
objects.description: Drinking cup intended for a single use
objects.extract: A disposable cup is a type of tableware and disposable food packaging. Disposable cup types include paper cups, plastic cups, and foam cups.

objects.title: Meerkat
objects.description: Species of mongoose from Southern Africa
objects.extract: The meerkat (Suricata suricatta) or suricate is a small mongoose found in southern Africa. It is characterised by a broad head, large eyes, a pointed snout, long legs, a thin tapering tail, and a brindled coat pattern.

objects.title: Bubble wrap
objects.extract: Bubble wrap is a pliable transparent plastic material commonly used for protecting fragile items during shipping. Known for its cushioning air-filled bubbles, it has also become a cultural icon, celebrated for its satisfying popping sound and alternative uses as a stress-relief tool.

objects.title: Bread loaf
objects.description: bread of the Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt (Egyptian New Kingdom), 1425–1353 BCE, in the collection of Museo Egizio, Turin, Italy (S.8295)

objects.title: Charivari (decorative chain)
objects.description: Piece of costume jewellery popular in the German state of Bavaria
objects.extract: Charivari (pronounced ) is a piece of costume jewellery popular in the German state of Bavaria. Made of solid silver or silver-plated chain, it is decorated with gemstones, coins, medals, horn, and small body parts from a variety of animals.

objects.title: Paper recycling
objects.description: Process by which waste paper is turned into new paper products
objects.extract: Paper recycling is the process by which waste paper is turned into new paper products.  After repeated processing the fibres become too short for the production of new paper, which is why virgin fibre (from sustainably farmed trees) is frequently added to the pulp recipe.

objects.title: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
objects.description: Loss of political control in antiquity
objects.extract: The fall of the Western Roman Empire, also called the fall of the Roman Empire or the fall of Rome, was the loss of central political control in the Western Roman Empire, a process in which the Empire failed to enforce its rule, and its vast territory was divided among several successor polities. The Roman Empire lost the strengths that had allowed it to exe

objects.title: Irrigation sprinkler
objects.description: Method of irrigating lawns and crops
objects.extract: An irrigation sprinkler (also known as a water sprinkler or simply a sprinkler) is a device used to irrigate (water) agricultural crops, lawns, landscapes, golf courses, and other areas. They are also used for cooling and for the control of airborne dust.

objects.title: Valpolicella Ripasso classico DOC
objects.description: type of the Valpolicella Italian controlled designation of origin

objects.title: Rangoli
objects.description: Colourful, traditional art form of India
objects.extract: Rangoli is an art form that originates from the Indian subcontinent, in which patterns are created on the floor or a tabletop using materials such as powdered limestone, red ochre, dry rice flour, coloured sand, quartz powder, flower petals, and coloured rocks. It is an everyday practice in some Hindu households; however, making it is mostly reserved for fes

objects.title: Backdraft
objects.description: Rapid or explosive burning of superheated gasses in a fire
objects.extract: A backdraft (North American English), backdraught (British English) or smoke explosion is the abrupt burning of superheated gases in a fire caused when oxygen rapidly enters a hot, oxygen-depleted environment; for example, when a window or door to an enclosed space is opened or broken. Backdrafts are typically seen as a blast of smoke and/or flame out of an

objects.title: Stone Age
objects.extract: The Stone Age was a broad prehistoric period during which stone was widely used to make stone tools with an edge, a point, or a percussion surface. The period lasted for roughly 3.4 million years and ended between 4000 BC and 2000 BC, with the advent of metalworking.

objects.title: Victorinox
objects.extract: Victorinox () is a knife manufacturer and watchmaker based in the town of Ibach, in the Canton of Schwyz, Switzerland. It is known for its Swiss Army knives.

objects.title: garlic peeler
objects.extract:
A garlic peeler is a kitchen utensil used to take off the skin off the garlic cloves.
A closed, hard-walled container, such as a jar or lidded tub or bowl, can be used to peel garlic.

objects.title: Bumper cars
objects.extract: Bumper cars or dodgems are the generic names for a type of flat amusement ride consisting of multiple small electrically powered cars which draw power from the floor or ceiling, and which are turned on and off remotely by an operator. They are also known as bumping cars, dodging cars and dashing cars.
objects.artist: Robert Taylor from Stirling, ON, Canada

objects.title: Morning dress
objects.description: Formal Western dress code for day attire
objects.extract: Morning dress, also known as formal day dress and the business suit, is the formal Western dress code for day attire, and business formal  consisting chiefly of a morning coat, waistcoat, and formal trousers for men, and an appropriate gown for women. The correct hat would be a formal top hat, or if on less spacious audience settings, optionally a collapsibl

objects.title: Weasel
objects.extract: Weasels  are mammals of the genus Mustela of the family Mustelidae. The genus Mustela includes the least weasels, polecats, stoats, ferrets, and European mink.

objects.title: Pale ale
objects.extract: Pale ale is a golden to amber coloured beer style brewed with pale malt. The term first appeared in England around 1703 for beers made from malts dried with high-carbon coke, which resulted in a lighter colour than other beers popular at that time.

objects.title: Lynn, Massachusetts
objects.description: City in Essex County, Massachusetts, US
objects.extract: Lynn is the eighth-largest municipality in Massachusetts, United States, and the largest city in Essex County. Situated on the Atlantic Ocean, 3.7 miles (6.0 km) north of the Boston city line at Suffolk Downs, Lynn is part of Greater Boston's urban inner core and is a major economic and cultural center of the North Shore.

objects.title: Turkish cuisine
objects.extract: Turkish cuisine (Turkish: Türk mutfağı) encompasses the traditional and regional foods of Turkey and its diaspora. It developed primarily from Ottoman cuisine, which synthesized Central Asian culinary traditions with Mediterranean and Middle Eastern influences.

objects.title: Yanmar
objects.extract: Yanmar Holdings Co., Ltd. (ヤンマーホールディングス株式会社, Yanmā Hōrudingusu Kabushiki-Gaisha) is a Japanese diesel engine, heavy machinery and agricultural machinery manufacturer founded in Osaka, Japan, in 1912.

objects.title: parasol
objects.description: umbrella specifically intended for protection from the sun, typically of lighter construction but sometimes inclusive of beach umbrellas etc.

objects.title: Leotard
objects.description: One-piece garment that covers the torso
objects.extract: A leotard () is a unisex one-piece skin-tight garment that covers the torso from the crotch to the shoulder. The garment was made famous by the French acrobatic performer Jules Léotard (1838–1870).

objects.title: Darkroom
objects.description: Room which can be made fully dark to allow for development of photographs and film
objects.extract: A darkroom is used to process photographic film, make prints and carry out other associated tasks. It is a room that can be made completely dark to allow the processing of light-sensitive photographic materials, including film and photographic paper.

objects.title: Squround
objects.description: Container with a shape between a square and a round tub
objects.extract: A squround, scround or sqround is a container with a shape between a square and a round tub. It resembles an oval but is sometimes closer to a rectangle with rounded corners.

objects.title: Cocktail glass
objects.description: Stemmed glass with an inverted cone bowl
objects.extract: A cocktail glass is a stemmed glass with an inverted cone bowl, mainly used to serve straight-up cocktails. The term cocktail glass is often used interchangeably with martini glass, despite their differing slightly.

objects.title: Bronze spearhead
objects.description: spearhead at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 74.51.5283)

objects.title: Asbestosis
objects.description: Pneumoconiosis caused by inhalation and retention of asbestos fibers
objects.extract: Asbestosis is long-term inflammation and scarring of the lungs due to asbestos fibers. Symptoms may include shortness of breath, cough, wheezing, and chest tightness.

objects.title: Cessna 172
objects.extract: The Cessna 172 Skyhawk is an American four-seat, single-engine, high wing, fixed-wing aircraft made by the Cessna Aircraft Company. First flown in 1955, more 172s have been built than any other aircraft.

objects.title: octopus salad
objects.description: Turkish entree dish, salad or meze of octopus

objects.title: Bulldozer
objects.description: Mobile machine which uses a frontal blade to push large volumes of material
objects.extract: A bulldozer or dozer (also called a crawler or Track-type Tractor) is a large tractor equipped with a metal blade at the front for pushing material (soil, sand, snow, rubble, or rock) during construction work. It travels most commonly on continuous tracks, though specialized models riding on large off-road tires are also produced.

objects.title: Valenki
objects.extract: Valenki (Russian: ва́ленки, [ˈvalʲɪnkʲɪ]; sing. valenok; Russian: ва́ленок, [ˈvalʲɪnək]) are traditional Russian winter boots without outer soles.

objects.title: Photo booth
objects.extract: A photo booth is a vending machine or modern kiosk that contains an automated, usually coin-operated, camera and film processor. Today, the vast majority of photo booths are digital.

objects.title: Tincture
objects.extract: A tincture is typically an extract of plant or animal material dissolved in ethanol (ethyl alcohol). Solvent concentrations of 25–60% are common, but may run as high as 90%.

objects.title: Salamander
objects.extract: Salamanders are a group of amphibians typically characterized by their lizard-like appearance, with slender bodies, blunt snouts, short limbs projecting at right angles to the body, and the presence of a tail in both larvae and adults. All ten extant salamander families are grouped together under the order Urodela, the sole surviving order from the group Cau

objects.title: Steaming
objects.extract: Steaming is a method of cooking using steam. In steaming, the food is fully immersed in steam, with no air present, so that the steam condenses on the food, and then the condensate drips down away from the food.

objects.title: List of inflatable manufactured goods
objects.description: Links to Wikipedia articles on notable classes of inflatable goods
objects.extract: This is a non-comprehensive list of inflatable manufactured goods, as no such list could ever completely contain all items that regularly change. An inflatable is an object that can typically be inflated with a gas, including air, hydrogen, helium and nitrogen.

objects.title: Octopus
objects.extract: An octopus (pl.: octopuses or octopodes) is a soft-bodied, eight-limbed mollusc of the order Octopoda (, ok-TOP-ə-də). The order consists of some 300 species and is grouped within the class Cephalopoda with squids, cuttlefish, and nautiloids.

objects.title: Durian
objects.extract: The durian ( ) is the edible fruit of several tree species belonging to the genus Durio. There are 30 recognised species, at least nine of which produce edible fruit.

objects.title: Diving equipment
objects.description: Equipment used to facilitate underwater diving
objects.extract: Diving equipment, or underwater diving equipment, is equipment used by underwater divers to make diving activities possible, easier, safer and/or more comfortable. This may be equipment primarily intended for this purpose, or equipment intended for other purposes which is found to be suitable for diving use.

objects.title: Oil lamp
objects.description: Lamp used for lighting by burning oil
objects.extract: An oil lamp is a lamp used to produce light continuously for a period of time using an oil-based fuel source. The use of oil lamps began thousands of years ago and continues to this day, although their use is less common in modern times.

objects.title: Fire extinguisher
objects.extract: A fire extinguisher is a handheld active fire protection device filled with extinguishing agents such as dry chemical powder, co2, water, etc. used to extinguish or control small fires, often in emergencies.

objects.title: Caninae
objects.extract: Caninae, whose members are known as canines (), is the only living subfamily within Canidae, alongside the extinct Borophaginae and Hesperocyoninae. They first appeared in North America, during the Oligocene around 35 million years ago, subsequently spreading to Asia and elsewhere in the Old World at the end of the Miocene, some 7 million to 8 million years

objects.title: Bronze aryballos
objects.description: bottle at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 00.13.3)

objects.title: Grasshopper
objects.description: Common name for a group of insects
objects.extract: Grasshoppers are a group of insects belonging to the suborder Caelifera. They are amongst what are possibly the most ancient living groups of chewing herbivorous insects, dating back to the early Triassic, around 250 million years ago.

objects.title: Frog
objects.extract: A frog is any member of a diverse and largely semiaquatic group of short-bodied, tailless amphibian vertebrates composing the order Anura (coming from the Ancient Greek ἀνούρα, literally 'without tail'). Frog species with rough skin texture due to wart-like parotoid glands tend to be called toads, but the distinction between frogs and toads is informal and p

objects.title: Food mill
objects.description: Kitchen utensil for mashing and sieving soft foods
objects.extract: A food mill (also called passatutto, purée sieve, moulinette, mouli légumes, passe-vite, or triturator) is a food preparation utensil for mashing and sieving soft foods invented in Brussels in 1928 by Victor Simon. Typically, a food mill consists of three parts: a bowl, a bottom plate with holes like those in a colander, and a crank fitted with a bent metal

objects.title: Ethernet
objects.extract: Ethernet ( EE-thər-net) is a family of wired computer networking standards designed for (but not limited to) local area networks (LAN), access networks, and metropolitan area networks (MAN). It was commercially introduced in 1980 and first standardized in 1983 as ECMA-82 and shortly after as IEEE 802.3.

objects.title: Washing machine
objects.extract: A washing machine (laundry machine, clothes washer, or washer) is a machine designed to launder clothing. The term is mostly applied to machines that use water.

objects.title: Khepresh
objects.extract: The khepresh (ḫprš) was an ancient Egyptian royal headdress. It is also known as the blue crown or war crown.

objects.title: Spile
objects.description: Peg used to control airflow in casks of ale
objects.extract: A spile (sometimes called a "cask peg") is a wooden or metal peg used to control the flow of air into, and carbon dioxide out of, a cask of ale or wine. Spiles can also be used to broach liquids (like maple sap) from a tree.

objects.title: Tapestry
objects.description: Form of textile art, traditionally woven on a vertical loom
objects.extract: Tapestry is a form of textile art which was traditionally woven by hand on a loom. Normally it is used to create images rather than patterns.

objects.title: Fire sprinkler
objects.description: Component that discharges water to protect buildings
objects.extract: A fire sprinkler or sprinkler head is the component of a fire sprinkler system that discharges water when the effects of a fire have been detected, such as when a predetermined temperature has been exceeded. Fire sprinklers are extensively used worldwide, with over 40 million sprinkler heads fitted each year.

objects.title: Hellenistic period
objects.description: Period of eastern Mediterranean history from 323 to 30 BC
objects.extract: In classical antiquity, the Hellenistic period covers the time in Greek, Eastern Mediterranean and Middle Eastern history following Classical Greece, between the death of Alexander the Great in 323 BC and the death of Cleopatra VII in 30 BC, in which all these regions were under the influence of Greek leadership, culture, and language as a result of Alexande

objects.title: Hubble Space Telescope
objects.extract: The Hubble Space Telescope (HST or Hubble) is a space telescope that was launched into low Earth orbit in 1990 and remains in operation. It was not the first space telescope, but it is one of the largest and most versatile, renowned as a vital research tool and as a public relations boon for astronomy.

objects.title: Jump seat
objects.extract: A jump seat (sometimes spelled jumpseat) is an auxiliary seat in an automobile, train or aircraft, typically folding or spring-loaded to collapse out of the way when not used.  The term originated in the United States c. 1860 for a movable carriage seat.
objects.artist: United States Senate - The Office of Mazie Hirono

objects.title: Blacklight
objects.description: Light fixture that emits long-wave ultraviolet light and very little visible light
objects.extract: A blacklight, also called a UV-A light, Wood's lamp, or ultraviolet light,  is a lamp that emits long-wave (UV-A) ultraviolet light and very little visible light.  One type of lamp has a violet filter material, either on the bulb or in a separate glass filter in the lamp housing, which blocks most visible light and allows through UV, so the lamp has a dim vi

objects.title: Dice
objects.description: Marked objects for finding random numbers
objects.extract: A die (plural: dice, sometimes also used as singular) is a small, throwable object with marked sides that can rest in multiple positions. Dice are used for generating random values, commonly as part of tabletop games, including dice games, board games, role-playing games, and games of chance.

objects.title: Totem pole
objects.description: Monumental carvings by Indigenous peoples of the Pacific Northwest
objects.extract: Totem poles (Haida: gyáaʼaang) are monumental carvings found in western Canada and the northwestern United States. They are a type of Indigenous Northwest Coast art, consisting of poles, posts or pillars, carved with symbols or figures.

objects.title: Woyzeck
objects.artist: Photographe : Michael Abril (Q107184279), pour le compte de l’Association québéc

objects.title: Barber chair
objects.description: Chair for customers of a barber
objects.extract: A barber armchair is a specialized chair for the customers of a barbershop. It allows the barber to manipulate the position of a customer for ease of cutting or otherwise treating hair.

objects.title: Dole Whip
objects.extract: Dole Whip (also known as Dole Soft Serve) is a soft serve dairy-free frozen dessert created by Dole Food Company in 1983. Prior to 2023, when Dole Whip began to be sold at retailers, Dole Whip was served at Disney theme parks and Dole Soft Serve was sold elsewhere.

objects.title: Bossaball
objects.extract: Bossaball is a team sport that originated in Brazil and was conceptualised by The Dutch Mart Eyckmans in 2004. Bossaball is a ball game between two teams, combining elements of volleyball, football, and gymnastics with music into a sport.

objects.title: Rosary
objects.extract: The Rosary (; Latin: rosarium, in the sense of "crown of roses" or "garland of roses"), formally known as the Psalter of Jesus and Mary (Latin: Psalterium Jesu et Mariae), also known as the Dominican Rosary (as distinct from other forms of rosary such as the Franciscan Crown, Bridgettine Rosary, Rosary of the Holy Wounds, etc.), refers to a set of prayers us

objects.title: Philips
objects.extract: Koninklijke Philips N.V. (lit. 'Royal Philips LLC'), simply branded Philips, is a Dutch multinational health technology and former consumer electronics company that was founded in Eindhoven in 1891. Since 1997, its world headquarters have been situated in Amsterdam, though the Benelux headquarters is still in Eindhoven.

objects.title: Vostok 1
objects.extract: Vostok 1 (Russian: Восток, lit. 'East'  or 'Orient') was the first spaceflight of the Vostok programme and the first human orbital spaceflight in history. The Vostok 3KA space capsule was launched from Baikonur Cosmodrome on 12 April 1961, with Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin aboard, making him the first human to reach orbital velocity around the Earth and to

objects.title: Rolex
objects.extract: Rolex ( ) is a Swiss luxury watchmaker and manufacturer based in Geneva, Switzerland. Founded in 1905 as Wilsdorf and Davis by German businessman Hans Wilsdorf and his eventual brother-in-law Alfred Davis in London, the company registered Rolex as the brand name of its watches in 1908 and became Rolex Watch Co.

objects.title: Copper
objects.extract: Copper is a chemical element; it has symbol Cu (from Latin cuprum) and atomic number 29. It is a soft, malleable, and ductile metal with very high thermal and electrical conductivity.
objects.artist:
Royal_Observatory_Edinburgh_East_Tower_2010.jpg: Chi And H
derivative work: Spin

objects.title: Manta ray
objects.extract: Manta rays are large rays belonging to the genus Mobula (formerly its own genus Manta). Three species are known: M. birostris, the largest at 7 m (23 ft) in width, M. yarae, which reaches 6 m (20 ft), and M. alfredi, the smallest at 5.5 m (18 ft).

objects.title: List of ursids
objects.extract: Ursidae is a family of mammals in the order Carnivora, which includes the giant panda, brown bear, and polar bear, and many other extant or extinct mammals. A member of this family is called a bear or an ursid.

objects.title: Spain
objects.description: Country in Southern and Western Europe
objects.extract: Spain, officially the Kingdom of Spain, is a country in Southern and Western Europe with territories in North Africa. Featuring the southernmost point of continental Europe, it is the largest country in Southern Europe and the fourth-most populous European Union (EU) member state.

objects.title: LDP 208
objects.description: small landing craft of the Portuguese Navy

objects.title: Hermes
objects.artist:
This picture belongs to Xavier Caré.
Please credit : Xavier Caré / Wikimedia Com

objects.title: Wooden fish
objects.extract: A wooden fish, also known as a muyu, moktak, mogeo, mokugyo, Chinese temple block, or wooden bell is a type of woodblock that originated from China that is used as a percussion instrument by monks and lay people in the Mahayana tradition of Buddhism.  They are used in Buddhist ceremonies in China, Korea, Japan, Vietnam and other Asian countries.

objects.title: Bicycle pump
objects.description: Air pump used for inflating bicycle tires
objects.extract: A bicycle pump is a type of positive-displacement air pump specifically designed for inflating bicycle tires.  It has a connection or adapter for use with one or both of the two most common types of valves used on bicycles, Schrader or Presta.

objects.title: Acetes americanus
objects.extract: Acetes americanus is a small shrimp species in the family Sergestidae found in the western Atlantic Ocean between Brazil and the United States.

objects.title: Rocket launcher
objects.description: Portable device that propels unguided rockets
objects.extract: A rocket launcher is a device, most often a weapon, that launches an unguided, rocket-propelled projectile. The projectile contains at least one component of what is called a warhead, which is usually explosive.

objects.title: Greater India
objects.description: Cultural sphere of India beyond the Indian subcontinent
objects.extract: Greater India, also known as the Indian cultural sphere, the Indic world, or the Hindu Cultural Sphere is an area composed of several countries and regions in South Asia, East Asia and Southeast Asia that were historically influenced by Indian culture, which itself formed from the various distinct indigenous cultures of the Indian subcontinent. It is an umbr

objects.title: Beer in New Zealand
objects.extract: Beer is the most popular alcoholic drink in New Zealand, accounting for 59% of available alcohol for sale in 2023, down from 65% in 2009. At around 61 litres per person per annum, New Zealand was ranked 27th in global beer consumption per capita in 2019.

objects.title: Spaceplane
objects.description: Spacecraft capable of aerodynamic flight in atmosphere
objects.extract: A spaceplane is a vehicle that can fly and glide as an aircraft in Earth's atmosphere and function as a spacecraft in outer space.  To do so, spaceplanes must incorporate features of both aircraft and spacecraft.

objects.title: Irrigation sprinkler
objects.description: Method of irrigating lawns and crops
objects.extract: An irrigation sprinkler (also known as a water sprinkler or simply a sprinkler) is a device used to irrigate (water) agricultural crops, lawns, landscapes, golf courses, and other areas. They are also used for cooling and for the control of airborne dust.

objects.title: Bus
objects.description: Large road vehicle for transporting people
objects.extract: A bus (contracted from omnibus, with variants multibus, motorbus, autobus, etc.) is a motor vehicle that carries significantly more passengers than an average car or van, but fewer than the average rail transport. It is most commonly used in public transport, but is also in use for charter purposes, or through private ownership.

objects.title: Mr. Monopoly
objects.description: Mascot for the board game Monopoly
objects.extract: Mr. Monopoly is the mascot of the board game Monopoly.

objects.title: Nebula
objects.extract: A nebula (Latin for 'cloud, fog'; pl. nebulae or nebulas) is a distinct luminescent part of interstellar medium, which can consist of ionized, neutral, or molecular hydrogen and also cosmic dust. Nebulae are often star-forming regions, such as the Pillars of Creation in the Eagle Nebula.

objects.title: Azulejo
objects.extract: Azulejo (Portuguese: [ɐzuˈle(j)ʒu, ɐzuˈlɐjʒu], Spanish: [aθuˈlexo]; from the Arabic الزليج, al-zillīj) is a form of Iberian painted tin-glazed ceramic tilework. They are an ornamental art form, but also had a specific function, such as aiding temperature control in homes.

objects.title: Folding screen
objects.extract: A folding screen, also known as pingfeng (Chinese: 屏風; pinyin: píngfēng), is a type of free-standing furniture consisting of several frames or panels, which are often connected by hinges or by other means. They have practical and decorative uses, and can be made in a variety of designs with different kinds of materials.

objects.title: Bodhrán
objects.extract: The bodhrán (, Irish: [ˈbˠəuɾˠaːnˠ]; plural bodhráin) is a  frame drum used in Irish music ranging from 25 to 65 cm (10–26 in) in diameter, with most drums measuring 35–45 cm (14–18 in). The sides of the drum are 9–20 cm (3+1⁄2–8 in) deep.

objects.title: NoCGV Svalbard
objects.extract: NoCGV Svalbard (W303) is a Norwegian Coast Guard icebreaker and offshore patrol vessel. The ship was constructed by Langsten at Tangen Verft shipyard in Kragerø and launched on 17 February 2001.

objects.title: Viking expansion
objects.extract: Viking expansion was the historical movement, beginning in the 8th century, which led Vikings to sail most of the North Atlantic, reaching south as far as North Africa and east as far as Russia, and through the Mediterranean as far as Constantinople and the Middle East, acting as looters, traders, colonists and mercenaries. To the west, Vikings under Leif Er

objects.title: Thomas Chippendale
objects.extract: Thomas Chippendale (June 1718 – 1779) was an English woodworker in London, designing furniture in the mid-Georgian, English Rococo, and Neoclassical styles. In 1754 he published a book of his designs in a trade catalogue titled The Gentleman and Cabinet Maker's Director—the most important collection of furniture designs published in England to that point whi

objects.title: Sauce Boat
objects.description: ceramic by Bristol Porcelain Factory (British); Worcester Porcelain Factory (British) (1972.188)

objects.title: Glossophaga commissarisi
objects.extract: Commissaris's long-tongued bat (Glossophaga commissarisi) is a bat species from South and Central America.

objects.title: Hipposideros vittatus
objects.extract: The striped leaf-nosed bat (Macronycteris vittata) is a species of bat native to eastern and southern Africa. It was formerly considered part of M. commersoni, which is now viewed as being restricted to Madagascar.

objects.title: Minttu
objects.extract: Minttu (Finnish for "mint") is a Finnish brand of peppermint-flavoured liqueur produced by Hartwall. A characteristic ingredient of the liqueur is peppermint, to give a fresh taste and aroma.

objects.title: Maize
objects.description: Species of grass cultivated as a food crop
objects.extract: Maize (; Zea mays), also known as corn, is a tall stout grass that produces cereal grain. The leafy stalk of the plant gives rise to male inflorescences or tassels which produce pollen, and female inflorescences called ears.

objects.title: play sculpture
objects.description: installation in Zollikofen in the canton of Bern, Switzerland

objects.title: Hot air balloon
objects.extract: A hot air balloon is a lighter-than-air aircraft consisting of a bag, called an envelope, which contains heated air. Suspended beneath is a gondola or wicker basket (in some long-distance or high-altitude balloons, a capsule), which carries passengers and a source of heat, in most cases an open flame caused by burning liquid propane.

objects.title: FOS.49
objects.description: Diplopterus macrocephalus fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Fire sprinkler
objects.description: Component that discharges water to protect buildings
objects.extract: A fire sprinkler or sprinkler head is the component of a fire sprinkler system that discharges water when the effects of a fire have been detected, such as when a predetermined temperature has been exceeded. Fire sprinklers are extensively used worldwide, with over 40 million sprinkler heads fitted each year.

objects.title: Tablet computer
objects.description: Mobile computer with integrated display, circuitry, and battery
objects.extract: A computer, commonly shortened to tablet or simply tab, is a mobile device, typically with a mobile operating system and touchscreen display processing circuitry, and a rechargeable battery in a single, thin and flat package. Tablets, being computers, have similar capabilities, but lack some input/output (I/O) abilities that others have.

objects.title: Chili pepper
objects.description: Varieties of pepper of the genus ''Capsicum''
objects.extract: Chili peppers, also spelled chile or chilli (from Classical Nahuatl  chīlli [ˈt͡ʃiːlːi] ), are varieties of berry-fruit plants from the genus Capsicum, which are members of the nightshade family Solanaceae, cultivated for their pungency (spicy heat). They are used as a spice to add pungency in many cuisines.

objects.title: Jug
objects.extract: A jug is a type of container commonly used to hold and serve liquids, but not normally to drink from directly. It has an opening, sometimes narrow, from which to pour or drink, and has a handle, and usually a pouring lip.

objects.title: Selena Gomez
objects.extract: Selena Marie Gomez (born July 22, 1992) is an American actress, singer, songwriter, businesswoman, and producer. Gomez began her career as a child actress on the children's television series Barney & Friends (2002–2004), and emerged as a teen idol for her leading role as Alex Russo on the Disney Channel sitcom Wizards of Waverly Place (2007–2012).
objects.artist: Anthony Quintano from Westminster, United States

objects.title: Pointed hat
objects.extract: Pointed hats have been a form of headgear of a wide range of cultures throughout history. Although often suggesting an ancient Indo-European tradition, they were also traditionally worn by women of Lapland, the Japanese, the Mi'kmaq people of Atlantic Canada, and the Huastecs of Veracruz and Aztec (e.g., as illustrated in the Codex Mendoza).

objects.title: Drottning Fredrika Dorotea Wilhelmina
objects.extract: HSwMS Carl XIII (originally launched as Drottning Fredrika Dorotea Vilhelmina) was a Swedish ship of the line in the Royal Swedish Navy. She was the second of three ships in the Konung Gustav IV Adolf class, alongside Konung Gustav IV Adolf (1799) (renamed Gustaf den Store in 1811) and Carl XIV Johan.

objects.title: Agriculture
objects.description: Cultivation of plants and animals to produce foods, fibers, fuels, and raw materials
objects.extract: Agriculture is the practice of cultivating the soil, planting, raising, and harvesting both food and non-food crops, as well as livestock production. Broader definitions also include forestry and aquaculture.

objects.title: Knitting needle
objects.extract: A knitting needle or knitting pin is a tool in hand-knitting to produce knitted fabrics.  They generally have a long shaft and taper at their end, but they are not nearly as sharp as sewing needles.

objects.title: Astrolabe
objects.extract: An astrolabe (Ancient Greek: ἀστρολάβος, romanized: astrolábos, lit. 'star-taker'; Arabic: ٱلأَسْطُرلاب, romanized: al-Asṭurlāb; Persian: ستاره‌یاب, romanized: Setāreyāb) is an astronomical instrument dating to ancient times. It serves as a star chart and physical model of the visible half-dome of the sky.

objects.title: Vacuum
objects.description: Space that is empty of matter
objects.extract: A vacuum (pl.: vacuums or vacua) is  space devoid of matter. The word is derived from the Latin adjective vacuus (neuter vacuum) meaning "vacant" or "void".

objects.title: Menthol
objects.description: Organic compound used as flavouring and analgesic
objects.extract: Menthol is a monoterpenoid organic compound that occurs naturally in the oils of certain plants in the mint family, such as corn mint and peppermint. It is a white or clear waxy crystalline substance that is solid at room temperature and melts slightly above.

objects.title: Odorrana grahami
objects.extract: Graham's frog (Odorrana grahami) – also known as the diskless-fingered odorous frog – is a species of frog in the family Ranidae. It is found in China (Sichuan, Yunnan, Guizhou, and possibly Hunan) and Vietnam (in Hoàng Liên National Park in the north).

objects.title: Auto rickshaw
objects.extract: An auto rickshaw is a motorized version of the pulled rickshaw or cycle rickshaw. Often called a tuk tuk, it is usually a three-wheeled vehicle, powered by an engine.

objects.title: Capybara
objects.extract: The capybara or greater capybara (Hydrochoerus hydrochaeris) is the largest living rodent, native to all countries in South America except Chile. It is a semiaquatic herbivore that inhabits savannas and dense forests, living near and in bodies of water and feeding mainly on grasses and aquatic plants.
objects.artist: Museum of Veterinary Anatomy FMVZ USP / Wagner Souza e Silva

objects.title: Dhol
objects.extract: Dhol (Hindi pronunciation: [ɖʰoːl]) can refer to any one of a number of similar types of double-headed drum widely used, with regional variations, throughout the Indian subcontinent. Its range of distribution in Indian subcontinent primarily includes northern areas such as the Jammu, Himachal, Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Kashmir, Sindh, Assam Valley, Uttarakhand

objects.title: Wibault 260
objects.description: French reconnaissance aircraft prototype of around 1930
objects.extract: The Wibault 260 R.2 was a contender for a French government contract for a long range, two seat reconnaissance aircraft, issued in 1928. There were eight prototypes in the 1931-2 contest and the Wibault was not selected for production.

objects.title: Boombox
objects.description: Portable music player with tape recorders and radio with a carrying handle
objects.extract: A boombox is a transistorized portable music player featuring one or two cassette tape players/recorders and AM/FM radio, generally with a carrying handle. Beginning in the mid-1990s, a CD player was often included.

objects.title: Coconut
objects.description: Species of plant in the palm family
objects.extract: The coconut (Cocos nucifera) is a member of the palm family (Arecaceae) and the only living species of the genus Cocos. The term "coconut" (or the archaic "cocoanut") can denote the whole coconut palm tree or the large hard fruit.

objects.title: Pearl
objects.description: Hard object produced within a living shelled mollusc
objects.extract: A pearl is a hard, glistening object produced within the soft tissue (specifically the mantle) of a living shelled mollusk or another animal, such as fossil  conulariids. Just like the shell of a mollusk, a pearl is composed of calcium carbonate (mainly aragonite or a mixture of aragonite and calcite) in minute crystalline form, which is deposited in concent

objects.title: Oscillating multi-tool
objects.description: Power tool which works by oscillation
objects.extract: An oscillating multi-tool or oscillating saw is a multitool and power tool that oscillates (rather than rotating or reciprocating), powered by battery or mains. The name "multi-tool" is a reference to the many functions that this tool can perform with the range of attachments available.

objects.title: Breakfast cereal
objects.extract:
Breakfast cereal is a category of food, including food products, made from processed cereal grains, that are eaten as part of breakfast or as a snack food, primarily in Western societies.
Although warm, cooked cereals like oat meal, maize grits, and wheat farina have the longest history as traditional breakfast foods, branded and ready-to-eat cold cereals (m

objects.title: Reconstructed clothing
objects.description: Clothing item redesigned into a new garment
objects.extract: Reconstructed clothing is used or vintage clothing that has been redesigned and resewn into a new garment. Reconstructed clothing falls under the umbrella of upcycling, as it adds value to discarded clothes through transformation.
